title: Verify on a real Termux device that test binaries link and pass
description:
The dev box can only 'cargo check' for aarch64-linux-android — there is no Android linker here, so the link step is unverified. The open question is cpal: its Android backend goes through ndk-sys, which links against libaaudio (/system/lib64/libaaudio.so). Termux's clang may not have that on its library search path by default.

Steps: on Termux, pkg install rust, then run
  cargo test --workspace
  cargo build --workspace --no-default-features   # CLI only

If linking against libaaudio fails, the fallback is to put cpal behind an optional 'audio' feature in the lingot crate and split lingot-tuner::core so Decimator/Analyzer (pure DSP, 4 tests) stay ungated — but do that only if it actually fails; per the hard rule cpal is NOT exempt.

title: Keep everything but the GUI compilable on Termux (aarch64-linux-android)
description: Standing hard rule, now in CLAUDE.md. eframe/winit pulls android-activity, which fails to compile for aarch64-linux-android. Fixed by declaring eframe under [target.'cfg(not(target_os = "android"))'.dependencies] and gating lingot_tuner::gui + main.rs on not(target_os="android"). cpal builds fine on Android via the ndk/AAudio backend. Verified: cargo check --target aarch64-linux-android --workspace --all-targets passes with default features; cargo test --workspace still 42 green natively.
closed_reason: eframe moved to a cfg(not(target_os=android)) target dependency; gui module and GUI main gated; android check + native tests both green.

title: Add CI job running the aarch64-linux-android check gate
description: CLAUDE.md now mandates 'cargo check --target aarch64-linux-android --workspace --all-targets' before every commit. That is currently enforced only by convention. Add it to CI (rustup target add aarch64-linux-android + the check) so a dependency that breaks the Android target is caught automatically rather than on someone's phone.
